title: mentha arvensis var. exigua
intro: Mentha arvensis var. exigua, commonly known as American wild mint, is a variety of the peppermint plant that grows in moist environments. This herb is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and headaches, and reduce inflammation due to its high concentration of menthol and menthone. The health benefits of American wild mint include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, and its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. American wild mint can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a range of applications.
